commands.name: discover-events
commands.title: Discover Events
commands.description: Browse and discover Luma events by location

commands.name: search-events
commands.title: Search Events
commands.description: Search for Luma events by keyword

tools.name: search-events
tools.title: Search Events
tools.description: Search for Luma events by keyword, topic, or interest. Use this to find events matching a specific query such as 'AI meetup', 'design workshop', 'hackathon', etc.

tools.name: get-place-events
tools.title: Get Place Events
tools.description: Get upcoming events for a specific city or place on Luma. Use this when the user asks about events in a specific location like 'San Francisco', 'Tokyo', 'Taipei', 'London', etc.

ai.instructions: You help users discover events on Luma. If a user asks about events in a specific city or place, call the `get-place-events` tool ONCE; if the user asks about events by topic or keyword, call the `search-events` tool. If the user's query contains multiple distinct topics or locations, call the appropriate tool for EACH topic or location to ensure complete results. Use the specific keywords or place names for each individual call. The tools return structured JSON with event details including a `startAt` ISO 8601 timestamp. IMPORTANT: If the user asks for events on a specific timeframe (e.g., 'today', 'tomorrow', 'next week', 'this weekend', or a specific date), you MUST first identify the current date from the context, then parse the `startAt` timestamp of each event from the tool response, and filter the results to include ONLY events that fall within the requested timeframe. You must EXCLUDE all other events from the output. Always output results as a Markdown table with appropriate columns such as Event Name, Date, Time, Location, and Link (if available). If no events match the filtered criteria, simply state that there are no events for that specific timeframe and do not output a table. Output ONLY the Markdown table or the no-events message, with no other text.

scripts.prepublishOnly:
echo "

It seems like you are trying to publish the Raycast extension to npm.

If you did intend to publish it to npm, remove the \`prepublishOnly\` script and rerun \`npm publish\` again.
If you wanted to publish it to the Raycast Store instead, use \`npm run publish\` instead.

" && exit 1
